message:
feat(conformance): add per-dtype tolerance tiers with violation reporting and regenerate test artifacts

Introduce a structured tolerance system in fj-conformance that follows
JAX's per-dtype conventions instead of using a single global tolerance:

  - F64/Complex128: tight (atol=1e-12, rtol=1e-10) for double-precision
  - F32/Complex64: moderate (atol=1e-5, rtol=1e-5) for single-precision
  - I32/I64/Bool: exact (0.0/0.0) since integer ops are deterministic

New types and infrastructure in crates/fj-conformance/src/lib.rs:
  - ToleranceTier: per-dtype tolerance struct with for_dtype(), exact(),
    and resolve() (takes wider of dtype default vs fixture override)
  - ToleranceViolation: per-element violation record with case_id,
    indices, expected/actual values, abs_diff, and tolerance_used
  - ToleranceReport: aggregated report with total_comparisons,
    violations list, max_abs_error, and mean_abs_error statistics
  - fixture_value_dtype(): infers DType from FixtureValue variants
  - compare_outputs_with_report(): extends compare_outputs with optional
    per-element tolerance recording for analysis
  - record_fixture_comparison(): decomposes scalar/vector fixture values
    into individual element-level comparisons for the report

Tests added for the tolerance tier system:
  - test_tolerance_f64_default, test_tolerance_f32_default
  - test_tolerance_i32_exact, test_tolerance_i64_exact, test_tolerance_bool_exact
  - test_tolerance_per_fixture_override, test_tolerance_erf_wider
  - test_tolerance_violation_report, test_tolerance_report_statistics
  - test_existing_fixtures_still_pass_with_tight_tolerances
  - test_fixture_value_dtype_inference

Widen tolerances for known-imprecise lax ops (erf, erfc, logistic):
  - capture_legacy_fixtures.py: add _wider_tol_ops set for ops needing
    1e-4 instead of 1e-6, applied per-case during fixture generation
  - legacy_transform_cases.v1.json: all lax_logistic and lax_erf/erfc
    cases updated from atol/rtol=1e-6 to 1e-4

Regenerated all test artifacts (144 files) reflecting the latest test
run after the vmap scalar-output constraint removal (45297fd):
  - artifacts/ci/runs/synthetic-forensics-test/manifest.json: updated
    timestamps and run_id for new synthetic CI run
  - artifacts/e2e/*.e2e.json (26 files): refreshed timestamps and minor
    duration_ms changes across all P2C001-P2C004 e2e test suites
  - artifacts/e2e/golden_journeys/*.golden.json (8 files): refreshed
    timestamps for all 8 golden journey scenarios (gj_01 through gj_08)
  - artifacts/testing/logs/fj-core/*.json (33 files): refreshed
    timestamps and phase_timings for fj-core unit test logs
  - artifacts/testing/logs/fj-interpreters/*.json (53 files): refreshed
    timestamps and phase_timings for partial_eval and staging test logs
  - artifacts/testing/logs/fj-trace/*.json (20 files): refreshed
    timestamps and phase_timings for trace/shape-inference test logs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
